dimensions.name: Visual Cohesion
dimensions.evidence: Consistent warm palette throughout: coral (#FF6B35), teal (#4ECDC4), lavender (#C7B8EA), warm background (#FFF9F0). Two Google fonts (Fredoka One for headings, Nunito for body) applied consistently. Card radii, shadow style, and spacing are uniform across all activity screens. Parent area uses a distinct indigo (#4F46E5) to differentiate it from the child area. No jarring visual inconsistencies found.

dimensions.name: Feature Completeness
dimensions.evidence: 19/22 acceptance criteria passed. FAILURES: (1) 20 of 100 Mandarin TTS audio files are OpenAI API error JSON responses (443 bytes each) — Mandarin audio broken for ~20% of vocabulary words. (2) ListenAndFind activity uses native browser new Audio() instead of Howler/pre-generated OpenAI TTS files for word playback, violating the 'NOT browser speechSynthesis' requirement. (3) GreetingStep has no audio playback — spec says 'greeting with audio' but only a visual greeting is shown.

dimensions.name: Responsive
dimensions.evidence: All pages use max-w-[428px] mx-auto constraining content to phone width on desktop. No display:none on landscape. Child screens constrain width but scroll naturally on wider viewports. Parent layout (slate-50 background) fills desktop properly. No orientation-lock or broken layouts detected at wider widths. Portrait-primary design works well.

dimensions.name: Navigation
dimensions.evidence: All 10 routes return HTTP 200: /, /onboarding, /session, /activities/explore-cards, /activities/listen-find, /activities/matching-pairs, /activities/song-time, /parent, /parent/dashboard, /parent/settings. The / route correctly redirects to /onboarding on first run or renders HomeScreen after setup. Note: there is no /home route (home is served at /); no /activities/listen-and-find route (it is /activities/listen-find). Internal links in HomeScreen are consistent. Back buttons and home button present in all activity screens.

dimensions.name: Data Persistence
dimensions.evidence: idb library (version 8.0.0) used correctly. DB initialized via openDB with 5 object stores: vocabulary (seeded with 100 words), progress, settings, sessions, songs. Singleton DB pattern via useDB hook prevents duplicate openDB calls. All writes (progress updates, session saves, settings) go through IndexedDB. Vocabulary seeded on first install upgrade callback. Progress, sessions, child name, age, PIN hash all stored in IDB.

dimensions.name: Error Handling
dimensions.evidence: Global error.tsx with retry + go home buttons. not-found.tsx with 404 content. All activity pages have loading states (animated panda), error states with go-home fallback, and empty-state handling. SongPlayer has audio-error screen with retry. AudioButton shows muted icon when audioError is true (though this is overly sticky — once any zh stub file causes a load error, the flag is global and stays set). VocabularyCard has onError fallback to /images/placeholder.png. Parent PIN page handles wrong attempts up to 3 with lockout and reset flow.

dimensions.name: Polish
dimensions.evidence: Framer Motion animations on all transitions (slide left/right between session steps, scale on tap, AnimatePresence for overlays). Confetti on celebration screen (canvas-confetti). PinPad shakes on wrong PIN. Idle timeout in ChildLayout redirects to home after 60s. Mascot idle animation floats continuously. Session greeting auto-advances after 3s. Mood check flows directly into word loading. Celebration overlay shows child name and word count. Song lyrics panel syncs with playback via RAF loop. Overall feel is polished and child-friendly.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: First-time setup flow: parent enters child name, selects age, sets 4-digit PIN, sees hand-device screen — under 2 minutes
acceptance_criteria.evidence: 5-step onboarding wizard at /onboarding: StepWelcome -> StepName -> StepAge -> StepPin -> StepHandoff. PIN hashed with SHA-256 + salt via Web Crypto API. Settings saved to IndexedDB. Flow is concise and can complete in under 2 minutes.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Child learning session: Start -> greeting with audio -> mood check -> 'Let us learn' -> Explore Cards -> celebration
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Session flow exists (GreetingStep -> MoodCheck -> ExploreCards -> CelebrationOverlay) but GreetingStep has NO audio playback. The spec requires 'greeting with audio' but GreetingStep.tsx has zero audio calls. Only a whoosh SFX plays on session start. The 'Let us learn' text is present visually but not accompanied by TTS audio.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Home screen with activity buttons: Explore Cards, Listen and Find, Matching Pairs, Song Time
acceptance_criteria.evidence: HomeScreen renders 4 activity buttons in a 2x2 grid with correct labels (both English and Mandarin), correct hrefs (/activities/explore-cards, /activities/listen-find, /activities/matching-pairs, /activities/song-time), and a Start Session CTA button.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Parent dashboard: PIN entry, words learned, streak calendar, stage progress
acceptance_criteria.evidence: /parent has PIN entry with PinPad (88px keys, shake animation on error, lockout after 3 wrong). /parent/dashboard shows words this week, streak count, StreakCalendar (7-day grid), Stage 1 label, and word list filterable by category.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Every vocabulary card shows: real illustration (DALL-E), English word, Mandarin word, audio play button
acceptance_criteria.evidence: VocabularyCard component renders: Image (1024x1024 DALL-E 3 PNG, verified real files), englishWord (36px Fredoka), mandarinWord (24px Nunito), pinyin (16px italic), AudioButton (88px circular button). All 100 vocabulary image files are real PNG images (100KB-1.1MB each). Fallback to /images/placeholder.png on error.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Tapping card plays English then Mandarin TTS audio (NOT browser speechSynthesis)
acceptance_criteria.evidence: ExploreCards and VocabularyCard use audioManager.playWord() which uses Howler.js correctly. However, ListenAndFind.tsx uses `new Audio(target.audioEnPath)` at line 57 and line 134 — native browser Audio API, not Howler. Additionally, 20 of 100 Mandarin audio files (/audio/zh/apple.mp3, banana.mp3, bird.mp3, blue.mp3, cat.mp3, dog.mp3, egg.mp3, eyes.mp3, fish.mp3, foot.mp3, green.mp3, hand.mp3, milk.mp3, mouth.mp3, nose.mp3, pink.mp3, rabbit.mp3, red.mp3, rice.mp3, yellow.mp3) are OpenAI API error JSON responses (443 bytes) — these will fail to play as audio.

generate-images.ts uses model: 'dall-e-3' with consistent kawaii toddler flashcard style prompt. All 100 vocabulary PNG files are real images (100KB-1.1MB, 1024x1024 RGB PNG format). Image generation script includes fallback to placeholder on error.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: OpenAI TTS audio files for words (nova voice)
acceptance_criteria.evidence: English audio: all 100 EN files are real MP3s (9-37KB each), generated with OpenAI TTS nova voice at speed 0.85. Mandarin audio: 80/100 zh files are real MP3s. 20 zh files (apple, banana, bird, blue, cat, dog, egg, eyes, fish, foot, green, hand, milk, mouth, nose, pink, rabbit, red, rice, yellow) are OpenAI API error JSON responses (443 bytes each, confirmed by hex inspection). These 20 words will fail to play Mandarin audio.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Sound effects: correct, incorrect, celebration, whoosh
acceptance_criteria.evidence: All 6 SFX files exist and are real MP3s: /audio/sfx/correct.mp3 (18KB), incorrect.mp3, celebration.mp3, whoosh.mp3, card-flip.mp3, tap.mp3. AudioManager.playEffect() plays them via Howler. Correct/incorrect played in ListenAndFind and MatchingPairs; whoosh plays on session start; celebration fires via CelebrationOverlay with canvas-confetti.

idb v8.0.0 used via openDB. Five stores: vocabulary (seeded on upgrade), progress, settings, sessions, songs. Singleton useDB hook. All user data (child name, age, PIN hash, word progress, sessions, song play counts) persisted to IDB. clearProgress/clearSessions for reset flow.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: ~100 words across Animals, Food, Colors, Body Parts, Family, Objects, Actions/Greetings
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Exactly 100 words in vocabulary-seed.ts: Animals (15), Food (15), Colors (10), Body Parts (10), Family (10), Objects (20), Actions/Greetings (20). All 7 required categories are represented.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Portrait orientation support, not broken on desktop (no display:none on landscape)
acceptance_criteria.evidence: No display:none or visibility:hidden based on orientation detected in any source file. All screens use max-w-[428px] mx-auto to center on desktop. Layout remains usable on wider viewports. No CSS orientation media queries that hide content.

Most tap targets comply: PinPad keys are 88x88px, AudioButton is 88x88px, activity grid buttons are min-h-[120px], Start Session is min-h-[88px], home button is 88x88px. VIOLATIONS: FlipCard (MatchingPairs) has explicit minWidth: 80, minHeight: 80 — below the 88px minimum. At 4-column board size on a 428px device, effective card size is ~90px (acceptable), but the hard-coded minimum is 80px which can be smaller on narrower screens or when 4 cols are used.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: No blank screens on any route
acceptance_criteria.evidence: All 10 routes return HTTP 200. Every page has loading states (animated panda), error states, and not-found page (404). SSR renders loading state immediately before client hydration. No blank/empty screens found on any route.

issues.description: 20 of 100 Mandarin TTS audio files (/audio/zh/*.mp3) are OpenAI API error JSON responses (443 bytes each, content: '{"error": {"message": "We could not parse the JSON body...'). Affected words: apple, banana, bird, blue, cat, dog, egg, eyes, fish, foot, green, hand, milk, mouth, nose, pink, rabbit, red, rice, yellow. When these files are loaded by Howler, a loaderror fires, audioManager.error becomes true, and the AudioButton permanently shows a muted/error icon for all subsequent audio interactions in the same session.
issues.suggestion: Re-run the audio generation script with a valid OPENAI_API_KEY to regenerate the 20 stub files. Use --force flag to overwrite existing stubs: npm run generate-assets.

issues.description: ListenAndFind.tsx uses native browser `new Audio()` API (lines 57 and 134) to play word audio, violating the non-negotiable requirement that audio must use pre-generated OpenAI TTS files via Howler, not browser speechSynthesis/Audio. The `new Audio()` API may not work reliably on iOS without user gesture unlock, does not benefit from Howler's caching/preloading, and does not use the audioManager singleton pattern.
issues.suggestion: Replace `new Audio(target.audioEnPath)` calls with audioManager.playWordEn(target.audioEnPath) or playWord(en, zh) via the useAudio hook, consistent with ExploreCards and SongPlayer.

issues.description: The spec requires 'greeting with audio' in the guided session flow. GreetingStep.tsx shows text and a mascot but plays no audio. The whoosh SFX fires in GuidedSession on step 0, but there is no TTS greeting spoken to the child. The child cannot hear 'Let's learn!' or any welcoming message.
issues.suggestion: Add audio playback in GreetingStep's useEffect — either play a pre-generated greeting TTS file or use audioManager.playEffect with a suitable audio cue. Consider adding /audio/en/lets-learn.mp3 and playing it on mount.

issues.description: The /audio/songs/ directory does not exist. All 3 songs (twinkle.mp3, old-macdonald.mp3, head-shoulders.mp3) referenced in songs-seed.ts are missing. SongPlayer handles this via Howler loaderror — the error state is shown with a retry button. While gracefully handled, Song Time activity is entirely non-functional since no song audio files are present.
issues.suggestion: Generate song MP3 files (royalty-free recordings or TTS-composed audio) and place them at /public/audio/songs/twinkle.mp3, old-macdonald.mp3, head-shoulders.mp3.

issues.description: FlipCard.tsx sets explicit style minWidth: 80, minHeight: 80 which is below the 88px minimum tap target requirement for toddler fingers. In practice, at the 4-column grid on a 428px device the effective size is ~90px, but the minimum constraint allows smaller cards on narrower screens.
issues.suggestion: Change minWidth and minHeight to 88 (or use min-h-[88px] min-w-[88px] Tailwind classes) to enforce the 88px minimum tap target across all viewport widths.

issues.description: AudioManager.error flag is never reset once set to true. After the first stub zh audio file fails to load, audioManager.hasError returns true permanently for the session. The AudioButton in ALL VocabularyCards then shows a muted/error icon even for words whose zh audio is fine. The error state should be per-word or reset on new playback attempts.
issues.suggestion: Track per-file errors in the cache Map instead of a global boolean, or implement a resetError() method called before each playWord attempt.

issues.description: The zh audio directory contains 20 extra files named with pinyin romanization (e.g., bizi.mp3, gou.mp3, mao.mp3, yanjing.mp3, zuiba.mp3 etc.) that are not referenced by any vocabulary entry. These appear to be artifacts from an earlier generation run using a different naming scheme. They add ~240KB of unused files.
issues.suggestion: Delete the 20 unreferenced pinyin-named files from /public/audio/zh/. The vocabulary seed references English-word-named files only.

issues.description: The /parent/dashboard route has no guard preventing direct navigation without PIN authentication. A user who knows the URL can access child learning data and settings directly without entering the PIN.
issues.suggestion: Add a session/auth check in the dashboard page — store a sessionStorage flag after successful PIN entry and redirect to /parent if not set.

summary: LittleBridge is a well-architected bilingual education app with strong visual cohesion, a complete 100-word vocabulary, functional IndexedDB persistence, proper Howler.js audio integration in most components, and polished Framer Motion animations. The build compiles cleanly with zero TypeScript errors. All 10 routes return 200 and render content. The major blocking issues are: (1) 20 of 100 Mandarin TTS audio files are corrupted API error responses that will silently fail during playback and permanently set an error flag on the AudioButton; (2) the ListenAndFind activity uses the native browser Audio API instead of the required Howler/pre-generated TTS approach; (3) the session greeting step plays no audio despite the spec requiring audio; and (4) all 3 nursery rhyme songs are entirely missing from the /audio/songs/ directory, making Song Time non-functional. These four P1 issues cause the verdict to fail. Once the zh audio files are regenerated, ListenAndFind is updated to use Howler, greeting audio is added, and song files are generated, the app would meet all acceptance criteria.
